ddls:
CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment of the server (e.g., production, development, testing) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the server */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the server (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last maintenance performed on the server */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Operating system version of the server */,
  "cpu_model" TEXT /* CPU model of the server */,
  "memory" TEXT /* Amount of RAM installed on the server */,
  "storage" TEXT /* Storage capacity of the server */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the setting belongs to */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., output_buffering, expose_php) */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the setting */,
  "current_value" TEXT /* Current value of the setting */,
  "tuning_reference_value" TEXT /* Recommended tuning value for the setting */,
  "remarks" TEXT /* Additional remarks or comments about the setting */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the setting (e.g., int, string, boolean) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the setting */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the setting is critical for server operation */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the setting */,
  "last_modified_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the setting was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_settings_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_settings_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "setting_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the setting the change was made to */,
  "previous_value" TEXT /* Previous value of the setting */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the setting */,
  "change_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the change was made */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the change */,
  "is_auto" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the change was made automatically by the system or manually by a user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_setting_history_setting_id FOREIGN KEY ("setting_id") REFERENCES settings ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_setting_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, developer, QA) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object or array of permissions assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
